completed_tasks.name: Nextcloud route fix (308 → direct serving)
completed_tasks.progress: Code + tests pass, NOT YET COMMITTED

remaining_tasks.name: Run litmus TDD cycle against stack

blockers.description: OnlyOffice mobile v9.3.1 crashes on WebDAV/Nextcloud login (LoginComponent null bug)
blockers.workaround: Use Cx File Explorer or litmus for automated testing instead

human_actions_pending.action: Install litmus: sudo apt install litmus
human_actions_pending.context: RFC 4918 compliance test suite needed for TDD cycle

decisions.decision: Replace 308 redirect with direct route serving for /remote.php/webdav/*
decisions.rationale: HTTP clients strip Authorization header on redirects, breaking auth for OnlyOffice and other Nextcloud-compatible clients

decisions.decision: Use litmus TDD cycle instead of manual mobile testing
decisions.rationale: User feedback: manual iteration too slow, need automated RFC compliance tests

decisions.decision: Use instance brice.localhost:8080 for testing (not alice)
decisions.rationale: alice has no physical storage directory on disk; brice has real files at /tmp/storage/brice.localhost:8080/

next_action: 1) Commit the Nextcloud route fix. 2) Install litmus. 3) Rebuild stack (go build -o /tmp/cozy-stack-webdav .). 4) Start stack + proxy for brice. 5) Run litmus against http://localhost:8888/remote.php/webdav/. 6) Fix failures in TDD cycle. This replaces manual phone testing.
context_notes: Phase 2 is verified and complete. The Nextcloud route fix is a bug discovered during manual testing — 5 files changed, all tests pass. The user wants to shift to litmus-driven TDD for Phase 3 and compliance validation. The proxy at /tmp/cozy-webdav-proxy.go rewrites Host header for multi-tenant routing (brice.localhost:8080). OnlyOffice mobile is broken (app bug), Cx File Explorer works but litmus is the right approach.
